---
title: "Data Viz Final Project: Pharmacies in Chicago"
format: html
---

```{python}
# load packages
import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
from shapely import wkt
#import pydeck as pdk
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import altair as alt
import streamlit as st
from matplotlib.patches import Patch
```

## LOADING AND PREPARING 2010 CENSUS GEODATA

```{python}
# load 2010 census data
#JESSICA'S FILE PATH:
#census_2010_gdf = gpd.read_file('C:\\Users\\blueb\\final_project_AWJP_w26\\data\\raw-data\\CensusTractsTIGER2010_20260303.geojson')

#ARIANNA'S FILE PATH:
census_2010_gdf = gpd.read_file('/Users/ariannawooten/Downloads/final_project_AWJP_w26/data/raw-data/CensusTractsTIGER2010_20260303.geojson')
```

```{python}
# count geometry by type
print('Geometry type:', census_2010_gdf.geom_type)

# count total number of features
print('\n N features:', len(census_2010_gdf))

# get crs
print('\n Coordinate reference system:', census_2010_gdf.crs)

census_2010_gdf.columns

```

```{python}
# create basic visualization (Chicago census tract map)
census_2010_gdf.plot().set_axis_off()
```

## LOADING AND PREPARING CHICAGO PHARMACY DATA
```{python}
# load data
#JESSICA'S DATA PATH
#data_path = 'C:\\Users\\blueb\\final_project_AWJP_w26\\data\\raw-data\\Pharmacy_Status_-_Historical_20260302.csv'

#ARIANNA'S DATA PATH
data_path = '/Users/ariannawooten/Downloads/final_project_AWJP_w26/data/raw-data/Pharmacy_Status_-_Historical_20260302.csv'

df_pharm = pd.read_csv(data_path)
```

```{python}
# explore data
df_pharm.shape
df_pharm.head()
```

```{python}
# check pharmacy status
df_pharm['Status'].unique()

# create mapping for pharmacy status
mapping = {
    'OPEN':'Open',
    'CLOSED': 'Closed',
    'Permanently closed': 'Closed',
    'closed': 'Closed',
    'open': 'Open',
    'permanently closed': 'Closed',
    'unknown': 'Unknown'
}

# recode the status column
df_pharm['Status'] = df_pharm['Status'].replace(mapping)
```

```{python}
# make pharmacy names capitalized
df_pharm['Pharmacy Name'] = df_pharm['Pharmacy Name'].str.capitalize()
```


```{python}
# load pharmacies as a geodataframe

# drop nas
df_pharm = df_pharm.dropna(subset=['New Georeferenced Column'])

# drop na strings
df_pharm = df_pharm[
    df_pharm['New Georeferenced Column']
    .str.lower()
    .ne('nan')
]

# convert wkt to a geometry
df_pharm['geometry'] = gpd.GeoSeries.from_wkt(
    df_pharm['New Georeferenced Column'],
    on_invalid='ignore'
)

# drop failed parses
df_pharm = df_pharm.dropna(subset=['geometry'])

# create geodataframe
pharm_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    df_pharm,
    geometry='geometry',
    crs='EPSG:4326'
)

# sanity check
print(df_pharm.shape)
print(df_pharm.head())
```

```{python}
# map colors to pharmacy status

# create color dictionary
color_dict = {
    'Open': 'green',
    'Closed': 'red',
    'Unknown': 'purple'
}

# map colors to gdf
pharm_gdf['color'] = pharm_gdf['Status'].map(color_dict)
```


## MERGING PHARMACY, HEALTH, AND CENSUS TRACT DATA

```{python}
# join pharmacy location and census tract gdfs (pharmacy emphasis)
combined1_gdf = gpd.sjoin(pharm_gdf, census_2010_gdf, 
    how='left',
    predicate='within')

# check object type
print('Object type:', type(combined1_gdf))

# check geometry type
print('\nGeometry type:', combined1_gdf.geom_type.value_counts())

combined1_gdf['geometry']
```

```{python}
assert (census_2010_gdf.crs == pharm_gdf.crs)
```

```{python}
# other sjoin option

# join gdfs (alternative merge with more census data and less pharm data)
combined2_gdf = gpd.sjoin(census_2010_gdf, pharm_gdf, 
    how='left',
    predicate='intersects')

# check object type
print('Object type:', type(combined2_gdf))

# check geometry type
print('\nGeometry type:', combined2_gdf.geom_type.value_counts())

#rename geo id column to merge with cha later
combined2_gdf = combined2_gdf.rename(columns={'geoid10':'GEOID'})

```

MERGE CHICAGO HEALTH ATLAS (CHA) AND PHARMACY DATA

```{python}
# Load CHA data
### ARIANNA'S CODE
cha = pd.read_csv('/Users/ariannawooten/Downloads/final_project_AWJP_w26/data/raw-data/Chicago Health Atlas Data Download - Census Tracts.csv')

### JESSICA'S CODE
#cha = pd.read_csv('C:\\Users\\blueb\\final_project_AWJP_w26\\data\\raw-data\\Chicago Health Atlas Data Download - Census Tracts.csv')

cha = cha.iloc[4:809] # remove non-data rows

# source: https://geopandas.org/en/stable/gallery/create_geopandas_from_pandas.html
cha_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    cha, geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(cha.Longitude, cha.Latitude), crs="EPSG:4326"
)

# rename combined_gdf1 column for spatial joining purposes
combined1_gdf.rename(columns={'geoid10':'GEOID'}, inplace=True)

# merge cha and pharmacy/tract data
cha_pharm = combined2_gdf.merge(cha_gdf, on='GEOID')


# merge cha and tract only data
#cha_tract = census_2010_gdf.merge(cha_gdf, on='GEOID')

# define geometry column for plotting later
#cha_pharm = cha_pharm.set_geometry('geometry_y')

print(f"shapes: merged = {cha_pharm.shape}, cha_gdf = {cha_gdf.shape}, combined1 = {combined1_gdf.shape}") # we lost about half of the cha data (n=805) and 10/421 of the other data

cha_pharm['geometry_x'] 

# i need to set this as geometry for future chloropleth maps
cha_pharm = cha_pharm.set_geometry('geometry_x')
```

## CREATE PLOTS

```{python}
# set up plot (Chicago Pharmacy Distribution on census map)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8,6))

# plot chicago in blue
census_2010_gdf.plot(ax=ax, color='lightblue', alpha=0.5)

# plot pharmacies
pharm_gdf.plot(
    ax=ax,
    column='Status',
    categorical=True,
    legend=True,
    markersize=5,
    alpha=0.7
)
plt.title('Chicago Pharmacy Distribution')
plt.tight_layout()

ax.set_axis_off()
plt.show()
```


```{python}
# group data by census tract
pharm_by_tract = (
    combined1_gdf
    .groupby('GEOID')
    .size()
    .reset_index(name='pharmacy_count')
)

# count pharmacies per census tract
pharm_by_tract.shape
```

```{python}
# count pharmacies per tract
pharm_counts = (
    combined1_gdf
    .groupby('tractce10')
    .size()
    .reset_index(name='pharmacy_count')
)
```

```{python}
# merge counts back to census polygons
census_and_pharm_gdf = census_2010_gdf.merge(
    pharm_counts,
    on='tractce10',
    how='left'
)

# fill missing tracts with zero pharmacies
census_and_pharm_gdf['pharmacy_count'] = (
    census_and_pharm_gdf['pharmacy_count']
    .fillna(0)
)
```

```{python}
# project to crs in meters
census_and_pharm_gdf = census_and_pharm_gdf.to_crs(epsg=26916)

# calculate area in square miles
census_and_pharm_gdf['area_sq_miles'] = (
    census_and_pharm_gdf.geometry.area / 2_589_988
)

# compute density
census_and_pharm_gdf['pharm_density'] = (
    census_and_pharm_gdf['pharmacy_count'] /
    census_and_pharm_gdf['area_sq_miles']
)
```


## Chloropleth Maps
```{python}
#make hardship values numeric
cha_pharm['HDX_2020-2024'] = pd.to_numeric(cha['HDX_2020-2024'], downcast=None)


# make choropleth map for CHA hardship index
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,10))

cha_pharm.plot(
    column='HDX_2020-2024',
    cmap='Blues',
    scheme='quantiles',
    k=5,
    legend=True,
 #  legend_kwds={'label': 'Pharmacies Per Square Mile'},
    linewidth=0.1,
    edgecolor='grey',
    ax=ax,
)

pharm_gdf.plot(
    ax=ax,
    color=pharm_gdf['color'],
    markersize=35,
    alpha=0.7,
    legend='status',
    edgecolor='white'
)


plt.tight_layout()
ax.set_title('Hardship Index Score\nChicago 2010 Census Tracts')
ax.axis('off')
ax.set_axis_off()


plt.show()
```

```{python}
combined1_gdf.head()
```

```{python}
# make choropleth map (pharm density per square mile)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,10))

census_and_pharm_gdf.plot(
    column='pharm_density',
    cmap='OrRd',
    scheme='quantiles',
    k=5,
    legend=True,
 #   legend_kwds={'label': 'Pharmacies Per Square Mile'},
    linewidth=0.1,
    edgecolor='grey',
    ax=ax
)

ax.set_title('Pharmacy Density per Square Mile\nChicago 2010 Census Tracts')
ax.axis('off')

plt.show()
```

POPULATION EXPERIMENTAL WORK (3/3 - 3:45pm)
```{python}
# load population csv
df_pop = pd.read_csv('C:\\Users\\blueb\\final_project_AWJP_w26\\data\\raw-data\\Population_by_2010_Census_Block_20260302.csv')

df_pop.head()
```




```{python}
# create a function to convert
def blocks_to_tracts(df_pop, geoid_col='CENSUS BLOCK FULL'): 

    # ensure geoid is string
    df_pop[geoid_col] = df_pop[geoid_col].astype(str).str.zfill(15)

    # extract tract geoid
    df_pop['geoid10'] = df_pop[geoid_col].str[:11]

    # keep only numeric columns for aggregation
    numeric_cols = df_pop.select_dtypes(include='number').columns

    # group by tract and sum numeric values
    pop_tract_df = (
        df_pop
        .groupby('geoid10')[numeric_cols]
        .sum()
        .reset_index()
    )

    return pop_tract_df
```



```{python}
# rename census block column in pop df
df_pop = df_pop.rename(columns={'CENSUS BLOCK FULL': ''})
```

```{python}
# merge population data to census gdf
census_gdf = census_gdf.merge(
    df_pop,
    on=''
)
```

```{python}
combined1_gdf.columns
```


```{python}
# load health data
df_health = pd.read_csv('C:\\Users\\blueb\\final_project_AWJP_w26\\data\\raw-data\\Chicago Health Atlas Data Download - Census Tracts.csv')
```

```{python}
# explore health data
df_health.shape
print(df_health.head)
print(df_health.columns)
```

```{python}
# employment based insurance
alt.Chart(df_health).mark_circle(size=60).encode(
    x=alt.X('Population:Q', title='Census Tract Population'),
    y=alt.Y('EMP_2020-2024:Q', title='Percentage of Residents With Employment-Based Insurance')
).properties(
    title='Employment-Based Insurance by Population',
    height=400,
    width=500
)
```

```{python}
# walkability
alt.Chart(df_health).mark_circle(size=60).encode(
    x=alt.X('Population:Q', title='Total Census Tract Population'),
    y=alt.Y('RITB_2022:Q', title='Walkability Ranking')
).properties(
    title='Walkability Ranking by Census Tract Population',
    height=400,
    width=500
)
```


```{python}
# save FULL gdf
# merge cha and pharmacy/tract data
full_gdf = combined2_gdf.merge(cha_gdf, on='GEOID', how='inner')


# merge cha and tract only data
#cha_tract = census_2010_gdf.merge(cha_gdf, on='GEOID')

# define geometry column for plotting later
#cha_pharm = cha_pharm.set_geometry('geometry_y')

print(f"shapes: full = {full_gdf.shape}") # 

full_gdf['geometry_x'] 

# i need to set this as geometry for future chloropleth maps
full_gdf = full_gdf.set_geometry('geometry_x')

print(full_gdf.head())
```



```{python}
# transit density and pharmacies

#make transit values numeric
cha_pharm['RITB_2022'] = pd.to_numeric(cha_pharm['RITB_2022'], downcast=None)

# make choropleth map (transit burden per census tract)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,10))

cha_pharm.plot(
    column='RITB_2022',
    cmap='Blues',
    scheme='quantiles',
    k=5,
    legend=True,
    linewidth=0.1,
    edgecolor='grey',
    ax=ax
)

pharm_gdf.plot(
    ax=ax,
    color=pharm_gdf['color'],
    markersize=35,
    alpha=0.7,
    legend='status',
    edgecolor='white'
)



# add legend title
leg = ax.get_legend()
leg.set_title("Relative Transportation Insecurity")
leg.set_loc('lower left')

ax.set_title('Transportation Burden per Census Tract\nWith Pharmacies in Chicago\nChicago 2010 Census Tracts')
ax.axis('off')
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()
```

```{python}
# plot per capita income and pharmacy density

# change per capita income variable from float to integer
cha_pharm['PCI_2020-2024'] = pd.to_numeric(cha_pharm['PCI_2020-2024'], downcast=None)

# make choropleth map (per capita income per census tract)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,10))

cha_pharm.plot(
    column='PCI_2020-2024',
    cmap='Blues',
    scheme='quantiles',
    k=5,
    legend=True,
    linewidth=0.1,
    edgecolor='grey',
    ax=ax
)

pharm_gdf.plot(
    ax=ax,
    color=pharm_gdf['color'],
    markersize=35,
    alpha=0.7,
    legend='status',
    edgecolor='white'
)

# add legend title
leg = ax.get_legend()
leg.set_title("Per Capita Income")
leg.set_loc('lower left')

ax.set_title('Per Capita Income per Census Tract\nWith Pharmacies in Chicago\nChicago 2010 Census Tracts')
ax.axis('off')
plt.tight_layout()

plt.show()
```

```{python}
# load gdf
census_2000_gdf = gpd.read_file('C:\\Users\\blueb\\final_project_AWJP_w26\\data\\raw-data\\Census_Tracts_20260302.geojson')
```

```{python}
# inspect data

# count geometry by type
print('Geometry type:', census_2000_gdf.geom_type)

# count total number of features
print('\n N features:', len(census_2000_gdf))

# get crs
print('\n Coordinate reference system:', census_2000_gdf.crs)

print(census_2000_gdf.head())
print(census_2000_gdf.columns)
```

```{python}
# create basic visualization
census_gdf.plot().set_axis_off()
```

```{python}
# join gdfs
combined3_gdf = gpd.sjoin(pharm_gdf, census_2000_gdf, 
    how='left',
    predicate='within')

# check object type
print('Object type:', type(combined3_gdf))

# check geometry type
print('\nGeometry type:', combined3_gdf.geom_type.value_counts())
```

```{python}
# group data by census tract
pharm_by_tract2 = (
    combined3_gdf
    .groupby('census_t_1')
    .size()
    .reset_index(name='pharmacy_count')
)

# count pharmacies per census tract
pharm_by_tract2.shape
```

```{python}
pharm_by_tract2.columns
```

```{python}
# count pharmacies per tract
pharm_counts2 = (
    combined3_gdf
    .groupby('tract_ce_3')
    .size()
    .reset_index(name='pharmacy_count')
)
```

```{python}
combined3_gdf.head()
```

```{python}
combined1_gdf.head()
```

```{python}
combined1_gdf.columns
```


```{python}
census_2000_gdf.columns
```

```{python}
census_2010_gdf.columns
```
